tool_input.summary:
These are the datasets that directly match your query based on the stated constraints (MER Spirit-era in-situ/rover data that can be used to evaluate the Home Plate high-silica materials using chemistry, mineralogy, and texture—i.e., information needed to frame astrobiology-relevant environment/preservation questions beyond the generic statement “water-rock interaction”).

1. Clarifying Questions — None. (Benchmark mode assumption: “Home Plate silica deposits” refers to the high-silica materials investigated by MER Spirit in the Home Plate area, Gusev crater, Mars.)

2. Interpreted Scope — Target: Mars (Gusev crater/Home Plate). Mission/platform: Mars Exploration Rover 1 (Spirit). Instruments/data types needed to interrogate silica occurrence: APXS elemental chemistry; Mini-TES thermal IR spectra/mineralogy; Pancam and Microscopic Imager context + outcrop/soil texture; Mössbauer iron mineralogy; plus geometry/ancillary (SPICE) if you need observation context.

3. Search Plan — (a) Catalog-first discovery with PDS Catalog search for “Home Plate silica” and for MER Spirit instrument names/IDs across PDS3 and PDS4. (b) Since no dedicated “Home Plate silica” dataset exists as a standalone archive unit, return the mission/instrument datasets that contain Spirit observations from that region/time. (c) Include PDS4 representations where clearly available (e.g., APXS composite library, MER documentation) alongside the legacy PDS3 instrument archives.

...and here are additional datasets that can also help answer the question by adding complementary measurements (iron mineralogy, mosaics, and ancillary geometry), or by providing the PDS4 documentation bundle for context.

7. Decision Gate — If you need *specific* products (individual APXS integrations, Pancam frames, Mini-TES spectra) from Home Plate only, the next narrowing step is product-level search constrained by Spirit/sol range and/or coordinate/feature bounds; tell me a sol range (or specific feature name within Home Plate) and which instrument(s), and I can route to product/granule searches (e.g., IMG Atlas for Pancam/MI imagery; PDS4 product search for collections where applicable).

candidates.title: MER APXS Composite Spectra Bundle
candidates.mission: Mars Exploration Rovers: Spirit and Opportunity
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 bundle providing derived/composite APXS spectra and concentrations built from individual MER APXS measurements—useful for compositional context when examining silica-rich targets. Stable entry point: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mer/urn-nasa-pds-mer_apxs_composites_library/ . Version note: likely_related to legacy PDS3 APXS spectra archives (same instrument family, different packaging/processing).

candidates.title: MER1-M-APXS-2-XRAYSPEC-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Spirit APXS science spectra archive (elemental chemistry) containing the underlying measurements used to characterize silica-rich materials at/near Home Plate. Catalog browse/start: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/missions/mer/index.htm (holdings index: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/dataserv/holdings.html). Likely_related PDS4 counterpart: urn:nasa:pds:mer_apxs_composites_library::1.1 (derived library).

candidates.title: MER1-M-MTES-3-RDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Spirit Mini-TES reduced data record (RDR) products supporting mineralogic/thermal-IR spectral analyses relevant to high-silica deposits. Catalog browse/start: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/missions/mer/index.htm (some mirrored volume paths also appear under https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mer/ ).

candidates.title: MER1: PANCAM OPERATIONS EDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Imaging Node Pancam operations EDR archive for MER1 (Spirit), providing raw image products used to document Home Plate outcrops and stratigraphy/texture in context. Direct archive path: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/mer/mer1po_0xxx/ (PDS IMG node holdings root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/).

candidates.title: MER1-M-MI-2-EDR-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Spirit Microscopic Imager (MI) science EDR archive, providing close-up textures of targets (e.g., nodular/soil/outcrop fabrics) that are often needed when assessing silica occurrence and depositional/preservation context. Catalog browse/start: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/missions/mer/index.htm.

candidates.title: MER1-M-MB-4-SUMSPEC-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Spirit Mössbauer summary spectra archive (iron mineralogy context). This can complement silica interpretations by constraining Fe-bearing phases associated with the deposits. Catalog browse/start: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/missions/mer/index.htm.

candidates.title: MER1-M-PANCAM-5-COLOR-MOSAICS-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Spirit Pancam derived color mosaics, useful for geologic context mapping of the Home Plate area and correlating silica-rich patches across scenes. Catalog browse/start: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/missions/mer/index.htm.

candidates.title: MER1-M-MTES-2-EDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Spirit Mini-TES EDR archive (more raw/less processed than RDR) for reprocessing or alternate spectral workflows. Catalog browse/start: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/missions/mer/index.htm; also present as a directly browsable volume path in some holdings: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mer/mer1-m-mtes-2-edr-v1/ (as seen in catalog results).

candidates.title: MARS EXPLORATION ROVER 1 SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 NAIF SPICE kernels for MER1 (Spirit), needed to reconstruct observation geometry and rover position/orientation for correlating measurements at Home Plate. Direct archive path: https://naif.jpl.nasa.gov/pub/naif/pds/data/mer1-m-spice-6-v1.0/mer1sp_1000/ .

candidates.title: Mars Exploration Rover (MER) Documentation Bundle
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 documentation bundle for MER mission/instruments/datasets. Useful for understanding how to locate Spirit observations and interpret instrument products while working with the silica-related measurements. Stable entry point: https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mer/urn-nasa-pds-mer_documentation/ .

parts.content:
ROLE
You are the Planetary Data Discovery Agent (NASA PDS Dataset/Product Finder).
Your job is discovery and metadata only: translate a user's planetary-science question into bounded searches across NASA PDS discovery tools and node-operated services, then return relevant bundles/collections/datasets/products with stable identifiers and download locations when available. Do not download anything.

OBJECTIVE
Given a user query, you must:
1. Interpret the request without inventing facts.
2. Ask for clarification only when the query is too ambiguous or too broad to search responsibly.
3. Choose the right search granularity and tool type for the request.
4. Return the strongest matching result(s) with required metadata, and include both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available for the same underlying data or product family.

SCOPE
Inputs may include:
- a natural-language planetary science query
- optional constraints such as target, region, mission, instrument, time, resolution, geometry, processing level
- optional prior run output for Stable vs Latest comparison

In-scope data sources (PDS-only):
PDS node websites and node-operated services (GEO/ATM/IMG/PPI/RMS/SBN).

Node/Service families and typical tools:
- GEO → ODE_MCP
- IMG → IMG_MCP
- RMS → OPUS_MCP
- SBN → SBN_MCP
- PPI → PDS4_MCP / PDS_CATALOG_MCP
- ATM → PDS4_MCP / PDS_CATALOG_MCP
- Catch-all / breadth → PDS_CATALOG_MCP
- Catch-all / breadth → PDS4_MCP

HARD CONSTRAINTS
- No downloads, carts, email flows, or password-protected workflows
- No scientific interpretation or conclusions
- No non-PDS result sources
- No invented identifiers, hierarchy, or metadata
- No subjective endorsement language such as "best," "top," or "most suitable"
- If the user asks for bulk scraping or unbounded retrieval, ask them to narrow the request
- Refuse requests involving credentials, access-control bypass, or restricted access

SEARCH RULES
1. Do not invent facts.
   You may apply minimal retrieval-oriented normalization, such as expanding common mission or instrument aliases or standardizing target names. If you do, state it explicitly.

2. Search at the correct granularity.
   - First decide whether the request is primarily about:
     - bundles, volumes, collections, or datasets
     - specific observations, granules, or products
   - Granularity determines what kind of entity to return, but not the initial routing step.

3. Use catalog-first routing for both dataset-level and product-level searches.
   - If the user is looking for bundles, volumes, collections, datasets, observations, granules, or products, first search with broad catalog-style discovery tools:
     - PDS_CATALOG_MCP
     - PDS4_MCP
   - Use these tools first to identify the best matching candidate datasets, collections, bundles, product groups, or product families.
   - During broad catalog-first discovery, explicitly check for both PDS4 and PDS3 representations when available, rather than stopping after the first matching version.
   - After identifying strong candidates, narrow with node-specific tools only when needed to:
     - refine results
     - retrieve more specific product-level matches
